classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Dramatic Improv for Comedians with Jerah Milligan
classes.description: Dramatic Improv for Comedians with Jerah Milligan This workshop will meet Thursday, July 23rd from 5pm-7pm at the iO Theater! Dramatic improv isn't about trying to be funny. It's about being truthful. You're creating scenes without a script by leaning into honest emotions, vulnerability, and real human behavior. The comedy, if it comes, comes naturally. Instructor Bio: Jerah Milligan is a force to be reckoned with in the entertainment world. Hailing from Washington D.C., this multi-talented actor, comedian, director, producer, and writer is carving out a name for himself as the next big thing. With a strong and unique comedic voice, Jerah is committed to telling stories that resonate. Jerah co-created and starred in Netflix's Astronomy Club , produced by the industry heavyweights Kenya Barris and Dan Powell, proving that his talent transcends the screen. On screen, his acting repertoire boasts credits in Netflix’s Black Mirror , Broad City , Blue Bloods , Chicago P.D. , and The Detour . Recently, he lit up Apple TV+’s Helpsters as Cheerleader Chadwick, and he’s made appearances in sketches for platforms like Above Average, Funny or Die, and College Humor. Jerah's dedication to his craft is evident in his extensive training at the Actor's Alliance, Sheila Gray Studios, The Groundlings, The Pit, and Upright Citizens Brigade. As a co-host of the podcast Black Men Can't Jump in Hollywood on the Headgum Network, he engages with vital conversations around representation in the industry. Together, he and his co-host have created a Snapchat series for Comedy Central and the web series Projecting for Seriously.tv.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Chicago Forms Sampler with James Dugan
classes.description: Chicago Forms Sampler with James Dugan This workshop will meet Friday, July 24th from 11am-1pm at the iO Theater! This workshop, taught by the incredible James Dugan, will provide students with a better understanding of the basic mechanics of various beloved longform improv formats that were originated and have been popularized in Chicago! Instructor Bio: James Dugan has been a staple of the Chicago improv scene since he moved here in 2010. He has performed with numerous Harold teams at iO, including Winter Formal and L'il Tooties. A veteran performer, he has sailed the seas performing on cruise ships with The Second City and was a member of TJ and Dave's Mission Theater ensemble. He has been teaching improv for more than ten years and can currently be seen performing with The Armando , James G. Gretchworth, Blueprint , Clued In: The Improvised Murder Mystery , and Hitchcocktails .

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Cook County Social Club Workshop with Mark Raterman
classes.description: Cook County Social Club Workshop with Mark Raterman This workshop will meet Friday, July 24th from 11am-1pm at the iO Theater! A workshop focused on finding fun quickly and often with your teammates! Cook County Social Club's iconic play style influenced generations of long form improvisers and improv teachers; gain insight into their chemistry and best practices in this one-of-a-kind workshop taught by founding member Mark Raterman. Cook County Social Club was named "Best Improv Group" by the Chicago Reader and a "Legendary improv team" by the Chicago Tribune. Their sold out show ran for over seven years at iO Chicago where their raucous, kinetic style of play quickly became a comedy fan favorite. Now performing at UCB LA, they are delighted to return to Chicago and the theater they love.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Narrative Longform with Molly Jones
classes.description: Narrative Longform with Molly Jones This workshop will meet Friday, July 24th from 1pm-4pm at the iO Theater! Narrative Longform expert Molly Jones shares the tips and tricks for narrative longform, storytelling, genre, and strong, sustainable characters. Instructor Bio: Molly Jones , originally from Los Angeles, has been performing in Chicago for 15 years. She has studied comedy at Second City, iO and The Annoyance. She performs in Clued-In, an improvised murder mystery at Second City and Hitchcock*tails at the Annoyance. At iO, she performs in Blueprint, as well as, the Armando Diaz experience, the longest running comedy show in the entire city. She also produces This Year in Music, an out-of-this-world variety show where performers from around the city celebrate a particular year of music from history.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Relationship Goals with Monique Moses
classes.description: Relationship Goals with Monique Moses This workshop will meet Friday, July 24th from 2pm-4pm at the iO Theater! Learn how to let your 'WHO' do double duty! In this workshop, participants work the skill of using RELATIONSHIP to inform strong initiations and build a more nuanced 'WHY'. For students who have taken a level one improv course and above. Instructor Bio: Monique Moses is a Canadian born, Emmy nominated producer, director, writer and actor based in Los Angeles. She has studied at comedy theaters UCB, The PIT and The Groundlings and has spent the last 12+ years performing, teaching and directing at her home theater UCB as well as venues and arts programs on both coasts. Notable shows and festivals include: Asssscat (UCB LA / NYC), Assssketch (UCBLA), Raaatscraps (Caveat, NYC), UCB presents Damned If You Do (Woolly Mammoth Theater, Washington, DC), Astronomy Club Presents: Let's Talk About Race Baby (UCB, NYC), The Story Pirates (The Kennedy Center, Washington, DC) and headlining / mainstage shows at UCB’S Del Close Marathon (NY / LA), New York Comedy Festival, Stumptown Improv Fest (Portland, OR), Steelstacks Comedy Fest (Bethlehem, PA) and Fliegende Funken Improv Festival (Bremen, Germany). In addition to her stage credits, Monique was co-Head Writer and a co-Executive Producer for Season 4 of HBO's A Black Lady Sketch Show and has consulted on projects with Jimmy Fallon, Mike Myers and John Mulaney. As a member of all black comedy team Astronomy Club, she co-created and starred in 'Astronomy Club The Sketch Show', which premiered on Netflix in 2019 to stellar reviews from Variety, Vulture and Rotten Tomatoes.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: "Exploring Intuition and Physicalization Through Jacques Lecoq and Viola Spolin" with SAND
classes.description: " Exploring Intuition and Physicalization Through Jacques Lecoq and Viola Spolin" with Thomas Kelly and Mike Brunlieb (from SAND) This workshop will meet Friday, July 24th from 3pm-5pm at the iO Theater! Mike has many years of experience working with the theater games of Viola Spolin. Spolin was a teacher and innovator hugely influential in the development of theatrical improvisation as an art form. Her radical approach is more relevant than ever right now, and offers an intuitive approach to improvisation and group work. Thomas is an expert in the pedagogy of Jacques Lecoq. Lecoq was an innovator in physical theater, buffon, and clown who sought to activate the body's intuition as a starting place instead of the mind. In this workshop, we'll explore their approach to space substance, accessing intuition, and theatrical communication. Come join and play some games! * Please wear clothes you will be comfortable moving around in * Instructor Bios: Thomas Kelly can be seen performing live in the Holy F*ck Comedy Hour in Chicago and around the world with the award winning improv ensemble, Sand. He can be seen on TV on shows like Showtime’s Work In Progress, NBC’s Chicago Med, NBC’s Shrink, Monuments, and in the stop motion short, Sleepy Steve. He is represented by Paonessa Talent LLC. Michael Brunlieb is a performer and teaching artist in Chicago. He improvises with the ensembles 'Sand', 'Real Angels', and '3 Actors Living in a Cave'. Mike has also performed in a handful of TV and film projects, including "Southside" (HBO), "Joe Pera Talks with You" (Adult Swim), and "Trial of the Chicago Seven" (Netflix). The core of Mike's teaching and performance is rooted in the work of Viola Spolin.

classes.title: iO Fest: Writing for SNL – Q&A with Claire McFadden
classes.description: Writing for SNL – Q&A with Claire McFadden This Q&A session will meet Friday, July 24th from 4pm-5:30pm in the Clybourn Event Space , upstairs at the iO Theater! Sit down with current SNL writer Claire McFadden for a Q&A session on her journey to become a writer at the legendary comedy institution and the current process for writing on the show! Instructor Bio: Claire McFadden is currently a staff writer at SNL. Before that, she spent two years on the Mainstage at Second City . And before that, she was a writer at Jackbox Games and a touring actor with the Second City National Touring Company. She wrote the short “Achiever” which premiered at SXSW in 2026. Her sketch duo Spooky Dookie will be performing at the Edinburgh Fringe this August 2026. She has performed at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival, San Francisco Sketch Fest, Out of Bounds, and Women in Comedy Festival, among others. Her web series “Framed” was an Official Selection of the New York Television Festival in 2018 and her web series “Denise Sells Houses” was an Official Selection in the Shorts Program at Just For Laughs in 2025.

classes.title: Disruptor: Volume 1 - Intro to Modern Clown
classes.description: Category: Featured Programs / Clowning

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Chicago-Style Organic Openings with Avery Ford
classes.description: Chicago-Style Organic Openings with Avery Ford This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th from 10am-1pm at the iO Theater! The Organic Opening is an art-form unto itself that scares even some of the most seasoned improvisers. This workshop provides valuable tools and tactics to help us navigate and, dare I say, come to love organic work. Learn to think less and support more in one of the most tricky but rewarding parts of the iO style! All proceeds of this workshop goes to the DARE Dropouts Scholarship which helps to put performers of color through the iO program! Instructor Bio: Avery Ford (He/Him) is an improviser, writer, comedian originally from Houston, Texas. He is honored to be serving as the Training Center Manager at iO. He currently plays on Pyramid Funnel, Chicago's current longest running "Deconstruction" team. He can be seen on iO House Team: D.A.R.E. Dropouts, The Spades Table, Blueprint, and HellMash. And when not at iO, he is traveling around the country performing sketch and improv as a member of the Second City's National Touring Company.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Improv for Writing with John Thibodeaux
classes.description: Improv for Writing with John Thibodeaux This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th from 10am-1pm at the iO Theater! Get out of your head, into your improv, and onto the page! How can improv help your writing? Over the course of 3 hours John will serve as a guide through multiple exercises designed to get you out of your head and let the creativity flow from stage to page. If you crave a toolbox for getting out of your own way so you can finally translate your inner voice into one that can live in a scripted work, this workshop is the one for you. Instructor Bio: John Thibodeaux has been improvising for over 20 years (including with iO Fest headliner, 3Peat) and writing TV comedy for 10. After training and performing extensively in Chicago, he headed out east where he wrote at The Late Show with Stephen Colbert and lives with his wife and two sons.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: "Game Of The Scene" with Caroline Martin
classes.description: " Game Of The Scene" with Caroline Martin This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th from 11am-1pm at the iO Theater! Students will receive a crash course in identifying and playing the game (what's funny) of the scene. They'll learn how to notice, create a pattern, and heighten the game of the scenes while building upon basic scene work. Instructor Bio: Caroline Martin is an improviser, writer, and actor. She co-created and starred in Astronomy Club: The Sketch Show on Netflix. She was a staff writer for The Tonight Show starring Jimmy Fallo n. She most recently worked on the Hulu show How To Die Alone . She is the winner of the 2014 NBC & UCB Diversity Scholarship Showcase, and 2016 Comedy Central NY Comedy Festival Comic to Watch. She performs and teaches improv, sketch, and late night writing at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater in Los Angeles and received a B.A. in Ethnic Studies from Brown University.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Coaching the Coaches – An Improv Seminar with Julia Morales
classes.description: Coaching the Coaches – An Improv Seminar with Julia Morales This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th from 12pm-2pm at the iO Theater! Looking to get into coaching or teaching improv? Or currently a teacher or coach, but looking to learn new tips and tricks? Then, this is the perfect improv seminar for you. Join veteran improviser and coach Julia Morales for this dive into coaching. How does one become a coach? Am I even ready to be a coach? How does one become a better coach? This workshop will be led by all around improv vet, Julia Morales, on the dos and don'ts of what to bring to the table when it's time for the student to become the teacher. Whether you're new to coaching or you're an old dog open to learning new tricks, this workshop will be an informative resource on how to approach various coaching scenarios. We will discuss topics such as how to set the tone for your session, building and understanding your own coaching philosophies, creating an effective curriculum for your students, and a lot more! Instructor Bio: Julia Morales (she/her) is a performer, writer, director, teacher, mentor, and supporter of all good things who is originally from Houston, Texas. She attended the University of Houston - School of Theatre & Dance and graduated from The American Musical and Dramatic Academy. She is an alum of the Second City Mainstage and an adjunct teacher and advisor at The Theatre School at DePaul University in the Comedy Studies program. She is also the former founder of Stepping Stone Theater and the 10,000 Hours program. She also has a vast involvement at various theaters and agencies in the Chicago community and thrives off of helping talented voices grow and excel, including in her role as the House Teams & Specialty Programming Director for the iO Theater. Jules is a master movie buff, a self care enthusiast, and an avid traveler.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Chicago Harold Symposium with Gretchen Eng
classes.description: Chicago Harold Symposium with Gretchen Eng This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th, from 2pm-4pm at the iO Theater! The Chicago Harold Symposium functions as a large group workshop – led by iO's Director of Education, Gretchen Eng – that takes any and all participants through the structure and best practices of the iO Theater's signature improv form, "The Harold!" Learn how to play this legendary improv form from the theater that fostered it into a classic. Explore thematic openings, group games, organic play, and the strength of group-mind! Instructor Bio: Gretchen Eng (she/her) is the Director of Education at the iO Theater and has performed in the Chicago comedy community since she was sixteen. She has been a performer, producer, and teacher at iO for over ten years, specifically with flagship shows Armando and Devil's Daughter , as well as teaching all levels of the iO Training Center and at improv festivals around the world. She served as a professor in Roosevelt University's Chicago College of Performing Arts for 7 years and has worked extensively for classical music organizations, such as Fourth Coast Ensemble and Lyric Opera of Chicago. She oversees professional development and Applied Improv for iO@Work at universities, companies, and non-profits around the world.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Intro to Prov-casting with "Improv is Dead"
classes.description: Intro to Prov-casting with "Improv is Dead" This workshop will meet Saturday, July 25th from 4pm-6pm at the iO Theater! Learn how to bring your yes and skills from the stage to a podcast in this cutting edge workshop! Get on a hot mic with IMPROV IS DEAD's Damian Anaya and Tim Lyons! In this 2 hour workshop, we'll work on hyper gift giving, can't show must tell, scene requests and our IMPROV IS DEAD form. You'll also get a copy of the class and scenes via mp3! Damian and Tim are members of FUDGE and hosts of IMPROV IS DEAD. They've been improvising together for around a decade and are known for being fast and funny, committing to raw emotion and extreme silliness while allowing the two to live simultaneously.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Dance Skills for Funny People with Justin Kimball (from Comedy Dance Chicago)
classes.description: Dance Skills for Funny People with Justin Kimball (from Comedy Dance Chicago) This workshop will meet Sunday, July 26th from 10am-1pm at the iO Theater! Designed for comedians, dancers, actors and movement-lovers of all levels, this workshop goes beyond silly walks (though we love those, too). You’ll generate physical, bold ideas through games, movement prompts, and sketch brainstorming sessions that turn “what if” into “oh no, we’re actually doing this.” Participants will: Collaboratively develop sketch premises and characters Choreograph and refine comedic dance moments as a group Learn tools and frameworks to create original work beyond the workshop Leave with a notebook full of ideas, a toolkit of creation strategies, and a stronger sense of how to make your audience laugh with your whole body. Come curious, ready to play, and dressed to move. *For this workshop, please bring movement clothes and notebook* Group Bio: Comedy Dance Chicago is a live performance company that blends sketch comedy, physical humor, and dance to create unique, highly entertaining, joyful shows for performing arts and comedy venues, schools, corporate events, and festivals. Our mission is to bring laughter, connection, and delight to audiences of all ages through high-energy comedy and dance performances. We aim to spark joy, lift spirits, and leave every audience walking away lighter than they arrived.

classes.title: Improv 101
classes.description: Category: Improv & Musical Improv

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: "ESCÁNDALO – Using Heritage, Culture, and Identity in Improv" with Luis Castelló
classes.description: " ESCÁNDALO – Using Heritage, Culture, and Identity in Improv" with Luis Castelló This workshop will meet Sunday, July 26th from 1pm-3pm at the iO Theater! Bringing your authentic self in improv is difficult when you’re the only player of color and/or when English is not one’s first language. Through this workshop, ESCANDALO teaches how to use emotional choices and physicality to become a strong performer who offers powerful gifts rooted in one’s culture. Whether you’re planning to perform a narrative improv show or just a single scene – learn how to harness your heritage, identity, and language to create funny and moving scene work.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Baby Wants Candy – Narrative Music Improv with Allen Lucas
classes.description: Baby Wants Candy – Narrative Music Improv with Allen Lucas This workshop will meet Sunday, July 26th from 3pm-5pm at the iO Theater! Baby Wants Candy is the world’s original and most celebrated musical improv comedy group, and in this workshop we’ll give you the tools you’ll need to improvise your own comedic musical. We’ll work on comedy basics, song forms, narrative structure, performance elements, and the improv techniques required to put on a made-up musical. You will learn the fundamentals of musical comedy improvisation in a fast paced, fun, supportive environment. It’s a ton of fun! Instructor Bio: Allen Lucas (he/him/his) is a comedian, musician, and educator who has been performing all around Chicago, Illinois since 2015. He holds degrees in Comedy Writing and Performance and Arts Management from Columbia College Chicago and has performed with many ensembles including Baby Wants Candy, Shamilton , Blueprint , and Marquee . He is also an improv instructor and the curriculum designer for the Music Improv Program at the iO Theater.

classes.title: iO Fest WORKSHOP: Solo Characters from Scratch with Andy Bolduc
classes.description: Solo Characters from Scratch with Andy Bolduc This workshop will meet Sunday, July 26th from 3:30pm-5:30pm In this workshop, you'll create original, showcase-ready solo characters from scratch, learning repeatable techniques to rapidly generate tons of ideas for characters, bring them to life using improvisational techniques, and revise them for maximum comedic impact. In the first half of the workshop, we'll walk through the character creation process from idea to structure to execution. In the second half, we'll flesh out our characters and hone them through individual feedback. By the end, you'll have the skills to build hilarious characters in your own voice and the confidence to blow audiences away. *For this workshop, please bring a pen and notebook* Instructor Bio: Andy Bolduc was a Second City Mainstage cast member from 2022 to 2025. In addition to teaching sketch and improv at iO, he co-hosts the podcast Idiotically Speaking with Chris Kattan and makes videos with his sketch group Cigarette Sandwich.

classes.title: Summer Comedy Camp (Ages 12-18)
classes.description: The Second City’s signature Comedy Camps for youth & teens are the perfect space for budding comedians or young people looking to spend the summer laughing and creating. Campers explore the fundamentals of improv as taught and performed at The Second City and develop skills like creative thinking, self-confidence, listening, brainstorming, team building, empathy, and communication, all while having loads of laughs. They’re introduced to basic scene structure, the art of storytelling and character development through the study of classic Second City scenes and other written materials. Mornings usually start off with improvisation before moving into comedy writing and specialty skills (like stand-up comedy or satire-writing or clowning) after lunch. There are also breaks built into the morning and afternoon sessions. Participants are encouraged to explore the freedom of movement and self-expression while also developing a better understanding of theatricality and onstage performance techniques. Every day includes exciting new activities! Coming from out of town? Visit: https://www.secondcity.com/training-center-where-to-stay-chicago/

classes.title: Summer Comedy Camp (Ages 7-11)
classes.description: The Second City’s signature Comedy Camps for kids are jam-packed with fun & games! Campers explore the fundamentals of improv as taught and performed at The Second City and develop skills like creative thinking, self-confidence, listening, brainstorming, team building, empathy, and communication, all while having loads of laughs. They’re introduced to basic scene structure, the art of storytelling and character development through the study of classic Second City scenes and other written materials. Mornings usually start off with improvisation before moving into comedy writing and specialty skills (like stand-up comedy or clowning) after lunch. There are also breaks built into the morning and afternoon sessions. Kids are encouraged to explore the freedom of movement and self-expression while also developing a better understanding of theatricality and onstage performance techniques. Every day includes exciting new activities!

classes.title: Reppin' It - Student Jam
classes.description: The SCTC Student Jam is a chance to learn some new improv games, play some old favorites, and get in some improv reps. All levels are welcome for a variety of exercises ranging from warmups, group exercises, two-person scenes, backline games, etc. Come to play or to watch! MUST BE A CURRENTLY REGISTERED STUDENT TO ATTEND.

classes.title: Headshot Dayz
classes.description: Need a new headshot or refresh? Look no further… Chicago-based photographer, Timothy Schmidt, brings more than a decade of experience shooting headshots and portraits for working actors, celebrities and various red carpet an music events. You’ve likely seen his work around The Second City, showcasing all of the stages and casts over the last decade, including mainstage, ETC., Toronto and New York. The Second City is now offering exclusive headshot sessions to currently enrolled students, where you will receive an “insiders” discount. This special price includes 3 professionally retouched studio headshot options and will be sent to you via a download link. To take advantage of this special rate, you will need to come dressed in your first-choice outfit and bring a second wardrobe option (i.e. Shirts, jackets, accessories, are best for a quick change). Please arrive camera ready with hair and makeup done. (Studio lights accentuate any oil on the skin so light foundation or powder is highly recommended for all.) Best practices are to show up 15 minutes early to give yourself a buffer and finalize your “last looks”. The more prepared you arrive, the better your shots will be! The photoshoot will be in studio on a white, gray, blue, or yellow backdrop. If you are interested in taking advantage of this offer, visit @timothymschmidt on Instagram or www.TimothyMSchmidt.com for examples of Tim’s work and to find out options for dates and times.

classes.title: WORKSHOP: The Invisible Clown – Improv and Clowning with Misha Usov
classes.description: The Invisible Clown – Clowning and Improvisation with Misha Usov This is a two-day workshop that will meet on Fridays July 31st and August 7th, from 9am-12pm. Learn to improvise like a clown, and clown like an improviser in this very special masterclass from one of the guest instructor Misha Usov, PRESS QUOTES ABOUT MISHA USOV: • "Smartly funny." — The New York Times • "Mikhail Usov's fishing boat idyll is a comic gem." — San Francisco Chronicle • "One of the world's best clowns." — Stuttgarter Nachrichten • "Ingenious comedy." — Frankfurter Allgemeine KEY HIGHLIGHTS & AWARDS: • 10-year Star of Cirque du Soleil (Created the iconic "Fisherman" character for the touring show 'Totem'). • Featured on The Tonight Show (USA), BBC (London), and Le Plus Grand Cabaret Du Monde (France). • Gold Medal Winner at the International Clown Festival (Ostend, Belgium). • Two-time Silver Medal Winner at the International Circus Festival (Saint-Paul-Les-Dax, France). • Author of the acclaimed book "The Invisible Clown" and founder of the Invisible Clown Institute.

classes.title: Improv 301
classes.description: Category: Improv & Musical Improv

classes.title: WORKSHOP: Teaching Improv with Gretchen Eng and James Dugan
classes.description: Teaching Improv with Gretchen Eng and James Dugan This workshop will meet Friday, July 31st from 3pm-5pm! iO instructors James Dugan and Gretchen Eng offer a crash course masterclass in improv pedagogy. Whether you're already a teacher, aspiring to coach, responsible for any type of classroom, or you just... feel great in improv class, James and Gretchen will offer insights into leading a group with compassion and confidence. This session will include example exercises, teaching turn-taking, and a brief Q&A. Instructor Bios: Gretchen Eng (she/her) is the Director of Education at the iO Theater and has performed in the Chicago comedy community since she was sixteen. She has been a performer, producer, and teacher at iO for over ten years, specifically with flagship shows Armando and Devil's Daughter , as well as teaching all levels of the iO Training Center and at improv festivals around the world. She served as a professor in Roosevelt University's Chicago College of Performing Arts for 7 years and has worked extensively for classical music organizations, such as Fourth Coast Ensemble and Lyric Opera of Chicago. She oversees professional development and Applied Improv for iO@Work at universities, companies, and non-profits around the world. James Dugan has been a staple of the Chicago improv scene since he moved here in 2010. He has performed with numerous Harold teams at iO, including Winter Formal and L'il Tooties. A veteran performer, he has sailed the seas performing on cruise ships with The Second City and was a member of TJ and Dave's Mission Theater ensemble. He has been teaching improv for more than ten years and can currently be seen performing with The Armando , James G. Gretchworth , Blueprint , Clued In: The Improvised Murder Mystery , and Hitchcocktails .

classes.title: DROP-IN: Improv with Gretchen Eng
classes.description: Join us for a weekly/warm/welcome intro to improv at the iO Theater guided by some of our favorite improvisers, coaches, and teachers. THEN stick around to see ANY not-sold-out show for FREE! Friday night every week, from 6-7:30pm in Studio A (upstairs, the green classroom).

classes.title: LGBTQIA+ Community Improv Jam
classes.description: LGBTQIA+ Community Improv Jams The Second City Training Center’s goal is to provide a safe environment for LGBTQ+ students across the larger improv community to come together and play freely. This event is free; no experience is required and participants do not have to be enrolled in a Second City class to participate. We do ask that those planning to attend register in advance for the event. The LGBTQIA+ Jam is for people who identify as Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Transgender, Queer, Intersex, and Asexual and all of the other identities not encompassed in the short acronym only and the jam will be hosted by a LGBTQIA+ faculty member.

classes.title: WORKSHOP: Improv for TTRPGs (Tabletop Role-Playing Games) with James Freetly
classes.description: Improv for TTRPGs with James Freetly This workshop will meet Saturday, August 1st from 11am-5pm at the iO Theater! There will be a one-hour lunch break from 1:30pm-2:30pm This class will increase the improv skills and confidence of tabletop roleplaying game players and game masters with sets of improv exercises focused on the unique context of roleplaying games, teaching techniques that support making choices that propel the story, playing characters with confidence, and helping other players shine! This class is ideal for a wide range of students, from casual players, to aspiring professional game masters, to actual play performers! Participants should be familiar with the basic mechanics of roleplaying games (e.g. the role of a game master, the role of a player, what a character sheet is, rolling polyhedral dice, etc). Previous improv training is not required. Instructor Bio: James Freetly has acted and improvised for The Second City Theatricals, iO, and The Annoyance. He has written for The Second City Network and holds an MFA in Prose & Poetry from Northwestern University. With over 20 years' experience running TTRPGs, he publishes adventures with GNAW Guild and has written and produced the actual play podcast Lakeshore & Limbo. He is secretly an IT project manager at Northwestern University.

classes.title: Writing Sadness Through Humor
classes.description: Cry ’til you laugh! Writing can help us make sense of our most difficult moments and sometimes, laughter helps us carry the weight. In this four-week in-person course, you’ll explore how to write about emotional experiences with both honesty and humor. Whether you’re drawing from your own life or creating fictional stories, you’ll learn how comedy can offer relief, insight, and connection. Together, we’ll talk about topics like sadness, anxiety, relationships, and conflict – whatever feels meaningful to you – and develop those ideas into written pieces. Along the way, we’ll look at comedians and writers who blend emotional truth with humor, examining how they shape their stories and discovering how you can shape your own voice. Through in-class writing, discussion, and supportive feedback, this course offers a space to process experiences, connect with others, and discover the unexpected joy of finding laughter in even the hardest moments. There’s no “right” way to express yourself here: just space to show up and write.

classes.title: Public Speaking
classes.description: Do you want to become a better public speaker? Do you feel nervous, unsure or even dread when you give presentations and speeches? This class will give you the tools and skills necessary to deliver effective and engaging speeches. You will learn and practice public speaking theory in a way that is fun and challenging. This class provides participants with a theoretical foundation of public speaking along with several opportunities to practice, refine and reinforce their learning. Develop greater public speaking success professionally and personally.

classes.title: DROP-IN: Improv to Sketch
classes.description: Join us for a monthly/warm/welcome intro to Improv to Sketch at the iO Theater guided by some of our favorite improvisers, directors, and teachers. You get a peek of what it is like to collaboratively generate sketch ideas and/or punch up material that you may already have. THEN stick around to see iO's premiere sketch show, Saturday Night Sketch, for FREE! The first Saturday of every month, from 6-7:30pm in Studio A (upstairs, the green classroom).

classes.title: WORKSHOP: Fluid and Organic Forms with Steve Nelson
classes.description: Fluid and Organic Forms with Steve Nelson This workshop will meet Sunday, August 2nd from 5pm-7pm! Everyone participates, all of the time. No sidelines, no waiting. Learn and practice forms that utilizes constant group engagement to create both scenes and stage picture. Through iO's signature approach to the collective (support!), create presentational organic work that flows seamlessly into and out of traditional scenes, each inspiring the other, while everyone remains active and onstage contributing. This workshop will cover skills and techniques relating to the forms Dragon's Breath, Bevy, and 4-Square. Instructor Bio: Steve Nelson is a performer, writer, and director based in Chicago. He's improvised with different teams in various styles at Annoyance, CIC, iO, LSI, The Playground, and Second City as well as produced sketch, plays, and podcasts. You can see him every Sunday at iO with Chicago's longest running Harold team – Deep Schwa – which he has been a part of for 14 years.

classes.title: Summer Comedy Camp (Ages 12-18)
classes.description: The Second City’s signature Comedy Camps for youth & teens are the perfect space for budding comedians or young people looking to spend the summer laughing and creating. Campers explore the fundamentals of improv as taught and performed at The Second City and develop skills like creative thinking, self-confidence, listening, brainstorming, team building, empathy, and communication, all while having loads of laughs. They’re introduced to basic scene structure, the art of storytelling and character development through the study of classic Second City scenes and other written materials. Mornings usually start off with improvisation before moving into comedy writing and specialty skills (like stand-up comedy or satire-writing or clowning) after lunch. There are also breaks built into the morning and afternoon sessions. Participants are encouraged to explore the freedom of movement and self-expression while also developing a better understanding of theatricality and onstage performance techniques. Every day includes exciting new activities! Coming from out of town? Visit: https://www.secondcity.com/training-center-where-to-stay-chicago/

classes.title: Summer Comedy Camp (Ages 7-11)
classes.description: The Second City’s signature Comedy Camps for kids are jam-packed with fun & games! Mornings usually start off with improvisation before moving into comedy writing and specialty skills (like stand-up comedy or clowning) after lunch. Kids are encouraged to explore the freedom of movement and self-expression while also developing a better understanding of theatricality and onstage performance techniques. Every day includes exciting new activities!

classes.title: AP1 - Mondays with Jill Mueller
classes.description: AP1: Chicago Improv for Beginners AP1 is an all-encompassing survey course that will give anyone a solid foundation in improvisational comedy – whether you want to perform, direct, write, or just know more. New to improv? This class is for you. AP1 introduces you to Chicago's approach to improv by covering a wide variety of concepts:​ The rules of Improv and breaking the rules Group mind (the myth, the facts) How to say "yes, and" and when to say “no” Finding or Creating the game Long-form Short-form Writing via improv Direction (taking and giving) Object and environment work Introducing a show This class will culminate in an optional student showcase at The Annoyance on the final Sunday of the term. Prerequisite: None

classes.title: AP2 - Mondays with Johanna Medrano
classes.description: AP2: Beginning Annoyance Philosophy - Initiations AP2 is an immersive examination of The Annoyance philosophy on, and approach to, improv. AP2 focuses on decisive initiations and a strong point of view. This class focuses on the first seconds of scenes and explores how they affect the rest of the scene. In AP2, you will cover: Personal responsibility in a scene − taking care of yourself and your choices Trusting instincts Creating/Finding “Your Deal” at the top of the scene Beginning character development Object work as inspiration Environment as inspiration The responsibility of operating without censorship. Individual feedback and tendencies This class will culminate in an optional student showcase at The Annoyance on the final Sunday of the term. Prerequisite: AP1 or sufficient background in improv. Please let us know in the comments section below where you have studied so that we may contact them to confirm.

classes.title: AP3 - Mondays with Jeff Quintana
classes.description: AP3: Scene Work AP3 is about taking that next step in scenes. While AP2 focused on the beginning moments of a scene, in AP3 students are challenged to play with more complexity and style, juggling it all within a scene while always maintaining their strong point of view. Aspects of this class include: Acknowledging the realities of your scene partner Scenic development: Finding the Highways and Bi-ways of a scene to extend its length Managing context without getting in your head (the who/what/where/when/why/etc.) Creating specific, surprising choices Playing with Opposites and Curve Balls Creating and playing with multiple patterns or games in a scene In-depth three person and group scene work Protecting and Playing the Freak Learning how to repair a scene that isn’t working Managing forms and style DaDa scenes (playing fiercely without needing to “make sense”) Justification: how to use it not out of fear but as a tool. Personal challenge and goals based on individual feedback Prerequisite : Students must have completed AP2 to take this course.

classes.title: AP5 - Mondays with Keenan Camp
classes.description: AP5 AP5 focuses on the creation of an original improv show inspired and devised by the ensemble. Exercises are tailored to help the improviser discover and strengthen their individual comedic improv voice and also on the “hows” of bringing this voice into an ensemble. classes.title: WORKSHOP: Advanced Harold Moves with Molly Jones
classes.description: Advanced Harold Moves with Molly Jones This workshop will meet Tuesday, August 4th from 7:30pm-9:30pm! With the help of instructor Molly Jones’ expertise, this workshop will teach you the improv moves that propel a show forward and tie our ideas together onstage. Have you been asking yourself “how do I elevate my improv game?” Are you looking to take your improv from just a collection of fantastic scenes to something that feels cohesive and makes bold, artistic statements? With the help of instructor Molly Jones’ expertise, this workshop will teach you the improv moves that propel a show forward and tie our ideas together onstage: including organic moves, thematic touches, a variety of stage pictures, and timing of connections/callbacks. You’ll discover how to identify and transform the moves of your teammates and how the smallest choices can return to enlighten the entire piece! Instructor Bio: Molly Jones , originally from Los Angeles, has been performing in Chicago for 15 years. She has studied comedy at Second City, iO and The Annoyance. She performs in Clued-In, an improvised murder mystery at Second City and Hitchcock*tails at the Annoyance. At iO, she performs in Blueprint, as well as, the Armando Diaz experience, the longest running comedy show in the entire city. She also produces This Year in Music, an out-of-this-world variety show where performers from around the city celebrate a particular year of music from history.

classes.title: IG1 - Wednesdays with Face Off Unlimited
classes.description: The Annoyance Training Center in Collaboration with Face Off Unlimited Present: Improvising Games I Mastering the art of game-based improvisation isn’t just about being funny, it’s about precision, adaptability, and commanding the mechanics of play at a professional level. Face Off Unlimited has spent years refining this craft, proving that game play isn’t just an aspect of improv, it’s a discipline all its own. This advanced course dismantles the outdated idea that structured game improvisation is only for beginners. Instead, students will develop the technical precision, spontaneity, and deep comedic instincts that make top-tier performers in this discipline unstoppable forces on stage. Through hands-on exercises and guided scene work, students will: Master the precision, nuance, and depth of game-based performance Improvise with structure without sacrificing authenticity or creativity Develop an audience-aware mindset to maximize engagement and surprise Refine performance choices through a professional lens Understand the business of game-based improvisation—and how to make it a career Whether you want to elevate your performance skills or position yourself for professional opportunities in high-level game play, this course will challenge and expand how you think about, and execute, improvised games. Levels: This course is divided into two levels. Improvising Games 1 (IG1) will focus on fundamentals of professional and high-level game play, "C to A" thinking, and introduce the Face Off Unlimited approach to improv. Improvising Games 2 (IG2) will delve deeper into the approaches introduced in IG1, while also focusing heavily on hosting, audience interaction and manipulation, and expanding the roster of improvised games mastered. Prerequisite: Students must have completed Improvising Games 1 Showcase: This class will culminate in an optional showcase on Saturday, October 3 - 5:30pm

classes.title: MI1 - Thursdays with Brody Lemke
classes.description: MI1: Scene Into Song Musical Improv I is an introduction to improvising songs, and the art of 'scene into song'. You'll learn how to sing structured songs, create catchy choruses that audiences remember, how to dance the same improvised dance move with seven other people, and how to express honest emotion while getting big laughs. This class will culminate in a one-night-only Musical Improv class show. YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE A GOOD SINGER TO BE A GREAT MUSICAL IMPROVISER! All you need is a supportive teacher to help you through the first few anxiety-ridden moments. I was never in choir growing up, and didn't think of myself as a 'singer'--I heard the vibrato of Broadway voices and thought 'I'm DEFINITELY not a singer. 'But it turns out I'm a GREAT singer--I just needed to improvise a few hundred songs to get VERY comfortable with my own voice. So whether you're interested in musical improv, or just want to feel more confident with your own voice, this class is for you. Prerequisite: Students must have completed AP3 or at least two improv classes at any theatre

classes.title: Summer Comedy Camp (Ages 12-18)
classes.description: The Second City’s signature Comedy Camps for youth & teens are the perfect space for budding comedians or young people looking to spend the summer laughing and creating. Campers explore the fundamentals of improv as taught and performed at The Second City and develop skills like creative thinking, self-confidence, listening, brainstorming, team building, empathy, and communication, all while having loads of laughs. They’re introduced to basic scene structure, the art of storytelling and character development through the study of classic Second City scenes and other written materials. Mornings usually start off with improvisation before moving into comedy writing and specialty skills (like stand-up comedy or satire-writing or clowning) after lunch. There are also breaks built into the morning and afternoon sessions. Participants are encouraged to explore the freedom of movement and self-expression while also developing a better understanding of theatricality and onstage performance techniques. Every day includes exciting new activities! Coming from out of town? Visit: https://www.secondcity.com/training-center-where-to-stay-chicago/

classes.title: Summer Intensive Part 1 August 10 - 14
classes.description: Annoyance 2026 Summer Intensives 5 day course - 6 hours per day with additional 2 hour workshops PART 1: The Annoyance Training Center teaches students how to develop and maintain a strong individual voice in their improvisation. These intensives will address the initiation of a scene; developing and maintaining strong characters; learning to hold on to your initial choices while at the same time, expanding on (heightening) the choices you have made; and learning to become deft at creating and weaving unexpected, character-based choices into your work. Annoyance classes challenge the conventional wisdom of the rules of improvisation, examining what’s behind them and how they came to be in the first place. Simply put, we make you the best improviser YOU can be. All of our intensive teachers are ensemble members who have learned and performed with The Annoyance, and thus have first hand experience with the way we approach improvisation. PART 2: Took the Intensive Part 1 and want more? Want to go further with your exploration of our philosophy? This is for you! With this intensive sequel, we go deeper into the teachings of The Annoyance, tackling things like context, group scene work, ensemble and much more. If you can only take one week, take part 1 . In The Annoyance Summer intensives, we try to cover all of the information in The Annoyance's AP1-5 program each day - AP1 on Monday, AP2 on Tuesday, and so on. These classes meet for 20 hours over 8 weeks. In these intensives, we're trying to cram that info into 6 hours over one day. A truly impossible task. classes.description: Annoyance 2026 Summer Intensives 5 day course - 6 hours per day with additional 2 hour workshops PART 1: The Annoyance Training Center teaches students how to develop and maintain a strong individual voice in their improvisation. These intensives will address the initiation of a scene; developing and maintaining strong characters; learning to hold on to your initial choices while at the same time, expanding on (heightening) the choices you have made; and learning to become deft at creating and weaving unexpected, character-based choices into your work. Annoyance classes challenge the conventional wisdom of the rules of improvisation, examining what’s behind them and how they came to be in the first place. Simply put, we make you the best improviser YOU can be. All of our intensive teachers are ensemble members who have learned and performed with The Annoyance, and thus have first hand experience with the way we approach improvisation. PART 2: Took the Intensive Part 1 and want more? Want to go further with your exploration of our philosophy? This is for you! With this intensive sequel, we go deeper into the teachings of The Annoyance, tackling things like context, group scene work, ensemble and much more. If you can only take one week, take part 1 . In The Annoyance Summer intensives, we try to cover all of the information in The Annoyance's AP1-5 program each day - AP1 on Monday, AP2 on Tuesday, and so on. classes.title: iO Improv Level 1: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24 – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 1 introduces students to all of the concepts necessary to become adept improvisers and performers. Students will learn how to build and apply the basic fundamentals of improvisation through a series of exercises, games, and storytelling. Level 1 is the appropriate starting point for the inexperienced student, but even those with some improv or theater experience will find value in the lessons of Level 1. The class will culminate in students learning and performing iO's signature piece, The Harold, one of the most sophisticated and rewarding forms of pure improv ever created. While 'Yes, And' is the guiding ethos in improvisation, students will go above and beyond that simple principle and learn how acceptance, agreement, honesty, connections, listening, and commitment will help them discover the truth in comedy. Our expert instructors foster a safe and joyous environment for students to play, experiment, and relish in the experimentation of improv. Level 1 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 2: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 2 expands upon the principles explored in Level 1 and teaches students the skills to make strong, bold and committed choices to elevate their characters, scenes, and performances. This level focuses on the importance of creating something artful and beautifully compelling on stage, challenging students to be more than just talking heads in their improvisation. Students will be engaged in exercises that encourage them to use physicality, environment, and stage picture to inspire their improvisation and raise the status of their improv from simple games and scenes to high performance art. The principles of Level 2 separate iO's training center from other improv schools by focusing on bold decision making and group support. Students will work together to commit to each other's choices and elevate those choices, creating truly unique and inspiring moments of improvisation. Level 2 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 3: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 3 redirects the focus back on to the essential building block of longform improvisation, the two person scene. The exercises in this level will help students slow down and find grounded real points of view and relationships to explore in their scenes. Rather than act like cartoon characters, Level 3 helps students understand that by listening and reacting to what someone is saying to them, they can create compelling and emotional scenes that will naturally evolve into comedy. Students will learn to develop the natural instincts that will help their scenes flourish and connect with audiences and will receive individualized feedback from their instructor about their strengths and opportunities for growth. 'Every line is golden and every detail is golden.' The principles of Level 3 help reassure students that they already have everything they need to create a great scene. Students will stop "inventing" and start truly improvising with guidance on how to respond with real emotions and committed character choices. Level 3 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 4: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 4 synthesizes the lessons from all of the previous levels and allows students to begin creating longform pieces that are cohesive, thoughtful, and artistic. Students will learn a variety of classic longform pieces in an effort to understand how structure can inform their art. Each form taught will offer students an opportunity to learn how a form can be a motivator of creativity and authenticity, and not just a rigid structure to adhere to. The skills in this class will help students perform longer sets and will encourage true improvisational exploration of ideas, concepts, and thoughts–urging students to actually say something with their art. Learn the rules, obey the rules, then break the rules. Additional focus will be given to iO's signature longform style, The Harold. Developed and performed at iO for the last 35 years, the Harold is now taught and performed all over the world and is a sophisticated and rewarding form that lends itself to all styles of improvisation. Students will learn the intricacies of performing the Harold with the understanding that the form is just a launching point for inspiration and not a destination in and of itself. Level 4 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

Inspired by their work in Level 4 and all the previous levels, students will work together under the guidance and assistance of their instructor to brainstorm and generate a new, unique form that will be performed on stage. At the end of this 8 week course, the students will spend the following 8 weeks performing their devised piece on stage at iO every Sunday night. classes.title: Improv 1
classes.description: Improv 1 introduces students to the fundamental concept of “Yes, And” in improvisation. Through group exercises and games, students will explore impulse & spontaneity, listening, being present in the moment, taking risks, finding agreement (making & accepting offers) and other basic building blocks of improvisation, all in a supportive environment that embraces the idea of trusting yourself and failing joyfully.

classes.title: Stand-Up 1
classes.description: In Stand Up 1 you will develop yourself as a solo comedic performer by writing and workshopping your own material. By understanding how earlier stand-up comics evolved, you will experiment outside your comfort zone and experiment until discovering your own unique style. Students will have regular writing assignments during the term. Class culminates in a final performance.

classes.title: Writing 1
classes.description: Writing 1 is for any student who is looking to start the journey of writing to and for a Second City-style revue. This class is the foundation for scenic comedy writing, a guide for utilizing an improviser’s skill set for rapid creation, and learning writing refinement. The concepts and tools provided in Writing 1 are the building blocks for being able to quickly and creatively write to and for a Second City-style revue. Every comedic writer must have the knowledge and skills that are offered in this course. Writing 1 teaches students how to write comedic scenes that could be found in a Second City-style revue by exploring different scenic set-ups, character dialogue, genres and point-of-view. Students use improv as a tool for character and dialogue generation. Writing 1 class time includes a combination of solo exercises and collaborative exercises to build skills in brainstorming, comedic structure and character development. Students also gain a Second City perspective on theatrical writing – including observation, idea generation, dialogue, and character creation. Feedback focuses on ‘what works’ in terms of the structural building blocks. Class time is active, positive and encouraging. Students practice concepts through a series of weekly homework assignments. Any student interested in creating Second City-style comedic writing or interested in extending their improvisation abilities and experiences into writing should take Writing 1.

classes.title: Intro to Acting
classes.description: The Second City Acting Program combines improvisational techniques with a variety of scripted media—including materials from the Second City archive—to help students build the foundations of grounded and compelling acting. Second City’s approach to sketch comedy is deeply rooted in behavior, relationships, character, and emotion. Over the years, our alumni have applied these principles across stage, film, and television to create performances that are not only hilarious but also heartwarming, dramatic, and award-winning. In this course, students will explore core acting techniques and methods for working with scripts. It’s a great fit for improvisers looking to refine their acting skills or for actors with little to no improv experience who want to stretch their creative muscles and expand their toolbox. Prerequisites: While some prior improv training is recommended, it is not required.

classes.title: Short Form Performance Ensemble
classes.description: Looking to play more shortform and enjoy the camaraderie of performing with a supportive group? Shortform Performance Ensemble offers space to return to your favorite games, try new ones, and strengthen your instincts through regular reps and low-pressure performance. This 7-week class builds on what you learned in Improv 5 or its equivalent, reinforcing skills like hosting, game set-ups, and audience interaction while introducing new shortform formats along the way. It’s about showing up, staying sharp, and enjoying the rhythm of weekly play with an ensemble you trust. Each week features ensemble-based rehearsal, feedback, and live performance prep, culminating in a final class show. No audition required – just a desire to connect, play, and perform. Prerequisites: Any of the following: Improv 5, IFA 2, or IFAX – Experienced Who it’s for: Improvisers who want to keep playing, build confidence, and have fun performing shortform with a supportive group. No audition required.

classes.title: Improv for Anxiety - Beginner
classes.description: Improv for Anxiety (IFAX) – Beginner A 7-week introduction to the art of improvisation and the tools it offers to help us deal with social anxiety. This innovative class uses ensemble-based improv to help people manage feelings of stress and anxiety. A Second City instructor will lead the class through a series of improv exercises that are specifically chosen to help students focus on the present, step outside of their comfort zones, and embrace the unknown in a welcoming and low-pressure environment. About The Second City’s Improv for Anxiety Program The Improv for Anxiety Program aims to build a nurturing space and student community where students can slowly explore the tenets of improvisation while receiving accommodations due to feelings of fear or anxiety. The Improv for Anxiety Program is not a substitute for therapy. Instructors focus on teaching improvisational skills such as listening, being others focused, taking risks, and working from a “Yes, And…” mindset.

classes.title: Intro to Writing for TV & Film
classes.description: Intro to Writing for TV & Film is for anyone who wants to learn the crucial basics of writing for film and television or anyone with a great idea of a movie who needs help putting it into (properly formatted) words. It gives you a strong foundation for TV and film writing, demystifies the language and process of writing, and gives you the tools to begin turning your great idea into the next Citizen Kane / Troop Beverly Hills (same thing). You’ll learn about the language of film/TV writing, the importance of deadlines, the unique qualities of a cinematic story versus a sketch, lots of screenplay formatting, how and what to pitch, and the scale of your ideas. You’ll watch films and TV shows with a critical ear for dialogue and structure, read scripts, learn how to pitch, pitch those great ideas, and create outlines or treatments for that million-dollar sitcom or film idea. Oh, and table reads! This course is a great introduction to the Comedy TV Pilots and Writing for Late Night programs.

classes.title: Music Improv 2-M: Sundays from 12-3pm

View all available days Level 2M invites you to explore more complex approaches to music improv, expressing the deeper meaning behind your songs and exploring more ways to collaborate and compose together as an ensemble. This level covers various different structures and styles of songs, and will allow you to further discover how your scenes can set the stage for emotional and compelling musical numbers. You will explore methods of finding the game of a song, making inspired musical choices, and bringing a sense of play and theatrics to their performances, which you will then display in an end-of-session showcase. Students who complete this level will be able to improvise songs in a variety of different styles while utilizing advanced techniques, and will deepen the connection between the story of their scenes and the purpose of their songs. classes.title: Musical Improv Fundamentals (Accelerated)
classes.description: This seven-week Musical Improv course combines our Level 1 and 2 Fundamentals classes into one term. Topics explored and practiced are melody building, rhyming, and improvising both simple and complex song structures; creating memorable solo and group songs from solid scenic choices; and working as a group to play both music improv games and long form pieces. There is no pre-requisite for the class.

classes.title: Storytelling 101
classes.description: Story is everything. Whether it’s inspiration for your next script to understanding yourself a bit better, learning what makes for a great story (and how to make it uniquely yours) is ultimately what allows us to connect, engage and understand what the heck this human experience actually is. No previous writing or performing experience is necessary. By the end of this course, you will have a strong collection of stories to choose from for wherever your storytelling journey should take you next. This course culminates in a performance of your story.

Feedback focuses on ‘what works’ in terms of the structural building blocks. Class time is active, positive and encouraging. Students practice concepts through a series of weekly homework assignments. Any student interested in creating Second City-style comedic writing or interested in extending their improvisation abilities and experiences into writing should take Writing 1.

classes.title: iO Improv: Advanced Harold
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. This level is an advanced post-graduate class of the iO program that offers students the chance to sharpen their skills further in iO's signature form. Advanced Harold uses what was learned in levels 4 and 5 of the iO program as a starting point to dig deeper into the form. The goal of this class is to go beyond just knowing how to do the Harold, but to get into the tactics that help you do the form in a successful and supportive way that is unique to you. This level also allows for a lot of personalized notes and diagnostics from the instructor and really helps students grow as individuals while also operating in an ensemble state of mind. Advanced Harold is an 8-week course that meets every Sunday from 3-5:30pm. Completing iO Improv Level 5 is a pre-requisite for this course. If you have any questions about this please reach out to classes@ioimprov.com! Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: Improv for Actors 1
classes.description: In today’s entertainment industry, improv and the ability to work without a script has become an essential tool not only for rehearsal, but for performance on screen and stage, as well as auditioning. Improv for Actors 1 is for people interested in how to be a better actor. IFA-1 is geared toward both working and post-grad actors who are looking to expand their craft with skills of improvisation. It’s also a great option for people with a theater degree or background in the dramatic arts to have fun with new people in a new environment. IFA-1 covers the fundamental elements of scene work, creating environment, and scenic transformation. Students acquire skills that can be applied to scripted and non-scripted work. Students become more improvisational in written moments and more confident when they improvise. Students work on building ensemble, object work, and the essence of a good improvisational scene. Using games, exercises, and other play, students explore relationships, character motivation, and heightening techniques to create compelling scenes and characters purely out of improv. Improv for Actors 1 is for students with a theater degree, currently enrolled in a full-length collegiate theater program, or have the equivalent amount of professional training and/or experience. Students must submit an acting résumé for consideration to be placed in IFA-1. Please submit a résumé through our online application.

classes.title: Intro to Acting
classes.description: The Second City Acting Program combines improvisational techniques with a variety of scripted media—including materials from the Second City archive—to help students build the foundations of grounded and compelling acting. Second City’s approach to sketch comedy is deeply rooted in behavior, relationships, character, and emotion. Over the years, our alumni have applied these principles across stage, film, and television to create performances that are not only hilarious but also heartwarming, dramatic, and award-winning. In this course, students will explore core acting techniques and methods for working with scripts. It’s a great fit for improvisers looking to refine their acting skills or for actors with little to no improv experience who want to stretch their creative muscles and expand their toolbox. Prerequisites: While some prior improv training is recommended, it is not required.

classes.description: In Stand Up 1 you will develop yourself as a solo comedic performer by writing and workshopping your own material. By understanding how earlier stand-up comics evolved, you will experiment outside your comfort zone and experiment until discovering your own unique style. Students will have regular writing assignments during the term. Class culminates in a final performance.

classes.title: Writing 1
classes.description: Writing 1 is for any student who is looking to start the journey of writing to and for a Second City-style revue. This class is the foundation for scenic comedy writing, a guide for utilizing an improviser’s skill set for rapid creation, and learning writing refinement. The concepts and tools provided in Writing 1 are the building blocks for being able to quickly and creatively write to and for a Second City-style revue. Every comedic writer must have the knowledge and skills that are offered in this course. Writing 1 teaches students how to write comedic scenes that could be found in a Second City-style revue by exploring different scenic set-ups, character dialogue, genres and point-of-view. Students use improv as a tool for character and dialogue generation. Writing 1 class time includes a combination of solo exercises and collaborative exercises to build skills in brainstorming, comedic structure and character development. Students also gain a Second City perspective on theatrical writing – including observation, idea generation, dialogue, and character creation. Feedback focuses on ‘what works’ in terms of the structural building blocks. Class time is active, positive and encouraging. Students practice concepts through a series of weekly homework assignments. Any student interested in creating Second City-style comedic writing or interested in extending their improvisation abilities and experiences into writing should take Writing 1.

classes.title: Accelerated Writing
classes.description: Accelerated Writing is an intensive course designed for experienced writers and advanced improvisers who are ready to dive into the core of sketch comedy writing. In just seven weeks, students will work through the material covered in the first two levels of Second City’s Writing Program, gaining the foundational structures and writing techniques needed to begin crafting their own theatrical sketch revues. This course is highly collaborative and fast-paced. Students should expect to engage deeply with in-class writing, partner assignments, and individual work between sessions. Writers will learn how to generate from abundance, develop strong comic characters, sharpen their comedic point of view, and create dynamic scenes in the style of Second City’s legacy of live comedy. Topics include: Generating ideas using a “yes, and” philosophy Creating strong comic characters Writing dynamic dialogue Introduction to satire Sketch structures and revue formatting Working in a writers’ room Revisions, feedback, and the role of the audience The course culminates in Open Stage: Sketch Takeover!, where students have the opportunity to hear their material read by student actors in front of a live audience. Students who successfully complete this course are eligible to bypass Writing 2 and enrol directly into Writing 3. Prerequisites: Acceptance into the Second City Conservatory, a Writing degree from an accredited institution, or permission from an artistic director Who it’s for: Experienced writers and advanced improvisers ready to build sketch revues and write in a collaborative, performance-focused environment

classes.title: Improv for Anxiety - Beginner
classes.description: Improv for Anxiety (IFAX) – Beginner A 7-week introduction to the art of improvisation and the tools it offers to help us deal with social anxiety. This innovative class uses ensemble-based improv to help people manage feelings of stress and anxiety. A Second City instructor will lead the class through a series of improv exercises that are specifically chosen to help students focus on the present, step outside of their comfort zones, and embrace the unknown in a welcoming and low-pressure environment. About The Second City’s Improv for Anxiety Program The Improv for Anxiety Program aims to build a nurturing space and student community where students can slowly explore the tenets of improvisation while receiving accommodations due to feelings of fear or anxiety. The Improv for Anxiety Program is not a substitute for therapy. Instructors focus on teaching improvisational skills such as listening, being others focused, taking risks, and working from a “Yes, And…” mindset.

classes.description: Advanced Workshop for Comedy Writers Comedy Pilots: Writers’ Room Intensive is an advanced-level workshop designed for writers with a completed or near-completed comedy pilot. This course simulates the collaborative environment of a television writers’ room, offering structured, weekly script readings followed by detailed feedback and discussion. Writers will analyze what’s working and what’s not—scene by scene, joke by joke—while receiving targeted suggestions for revisions and punch-ups. The focus is on refining the script through peer critique, rewriting, and group brainstorming, all in a supportive, professional-style setting. Ideal for writers who are serious about getting their pilot script stage- or pitch-ready.

classes.title: Improv (Ages 11-13)
classes.description: Improv for ages 11-13 is for kids who want an introduction to improvisation. This class uses the tenets of improv like agreement, listening, and emotions to teach kids basic life skills. It gets them out of their heads and active while showing them new ways they can work with and learn from others. Students meet kids from other schools and neighborhoods and form lasting friendships. This class provides an opportunity to create improv and work with kids in activities that are not sports related. This class teaches the building blocks of basic improv and introduces kids to the fundamental aspects of stage craft. The games and exercises develop emotional intelligence. The focus here is put on physical expression as well as verbal communication. The kids will learn how to work on a big project and have fun with others. Students will be led through some basic improv exercises and games. These are meant to be fun and focus on communication and teamwork. Kids will work on being a good ensemble member as well as developing their individual skills in a low-pressure environment. There are some aspects of performing involved, but students are not asked to perform on stage. This course is intended for students in middle school, grades 6-8. On the last day of class, the final 15-20 minutes of class time feature an in-class presentation by students that parents/guardians are welcome to attend. These are in-classroom and not a ticketed event, so space is very limited. Dates and times subject to change.

classes.title: High School Ensemble (Grades 9-12) (Apple)
classes.description: The Teen Ensemble is for students ages 14-18 who have improv and/or acting experience and want to develop and perform in an original show with an ensemble. This class gives you the full experience of developing a Second City revue. Students will experience the thrills and frustrations that come from creating something from nothing. It is a great experience that teaches many life lessons. Students will walk away having accomplished something that not many people have done. This is the highest-level performance class for youth and teens so students will build on skills they have already learned. There is still opportunity to learn new things depending on the individual student’s background. For example, if a student has a background in improv they will learn more about acting or music. There are also many life lessons to be learned about working closely with other people and performing in front of a crowd. This class involves creating an approximately twenty-five minute long original Second City-style revue. You will work within an ensemble, you will create songs with a Musical Director, and you will develop scenes and play performance-level improv games. You will also perform your original revue in a Training Center theater. No audition is required but students must have at least two terms of classes or 2 summer camps with the Second City or equivalent experience. Please use this form to submit your interest. Please note these performances happen outside of class time

classes.title: Fall Intensive Part 1 September 14-18
classes.description: Annoyance 2026 Fall Intensives 5 day course - 6 hours per day with additional 2 hour workshops PART 1: The Annoyance Training Center teaches students how to develop and maintain a strong individual voice in their improvisation. These intensives will address the initiation of a scene; developing and maintaining strong characters; learning to hold on to your initial choices while at the same time, expanding on (heightening) the choices you have made; and learning to become deft at creating and weaving unexpected, character-based choices into your work. Annoyance classes challenge the conventional wisdom of the rules of improvisation, examining what’s behind them and how they came to be in the first place. Simply put, we make you the best improviser YOU can be. All of our intensive teachers are ensemble members who have learned and performed with The Annoyance, and thus have first hand experience with the way we approach improvisation. PART 2: Took the Intensive Part 1 and want more? Want to go further with your exploration of our philosophy? This is for you! With this intensive sequel, we go deeper into the teachings of The Annoyance, tackling things like context, group scene work, ensemble and much more. If you can only take one week, take part 1 . In The Annoyance Intensives, we try to cover all of the information in The Annoyance's AP1-5 program each day - AP1 on Monday, AP2 on Tuesday, and so on. These classes meet for 20 hours over 8 weeks. In these intensives, we're trying to cram that info into 6 hours over one day. A truly impossible task. So, part 2 was created as a way to convey more of the information we couldn't get to in part 1, while also allowing instructors to get deeper into concepts they're passionate about, knowing the students all have at least part 1 under their belt. PREREQUISITE FOR PART 2: Students must have previously completed an Annoyance Part 1 Intensive or have completed AP5 within The Annoyance Training Center SCHEDULE: Classes: Mon - Fri 10am-5pm with a 1 hour break for lunch (1-2pm) Optional Workshops: Mon - Thu 5-7pm Optional Showcase: Fri 6pm PAST PART 1 WORKSHOPS: Advanced Improv with Mick Napier Game-Based Improv with Face Off Unlimited (FOU) Musical Improv with Tuesday Musical Improv (TMI) Genre-Based Improv with Hitch*Cocktails Please Disregard Dates and Schedule Listed Below (Those pertain to our non-intensive classes)
