classes.description: Looking to start your improv journey but not ready to commit to a full term of classes? Intro to Improv offers a fun and low pressure way to dive into the basics of improvisation. In this 4-week course, you’ll explore essential skills like saying “Yes, And,” making offers, and active listening. You’ll also start building trust with your ensemble and discover the joy of spontaneity and creative risk-taking. This class is designed to give you a solid foundation while fitting into your schedule.

classes.title: Intro to Writing
classes.description: Looking to start your comedy writing journey – but not ready to commit to a full term of classes? Intro to Writing offers a flexible, 4-week introduction to the fundamentals of sketch comedy writing. You’ll learn how to generate original ideas, identify strong comic premises, and build simple sketch outlines. Each week, you’ll practice writing on your own and with others, experiment with character and dialogue, and start to understand what makes a scene work.

classes.title: Public Speaking
classes.description: Do you want to become a better public speaker? Do you feel nervous, unsure or even dread when you give presentations and speeches? This class will give you the tools and skills necessary to deliver effective and engaging speeches. You will learn and practice public speaking theory in a way that is fun and challenging. This class provides participants with a theoretical foundation of public speaking along with several opportunities to practice, refine and reinforce their learning. Develop greater public speaking success professionally and personally.

classes.title: Fall Intensive Part 1 October 5 - 9
classes.description: Annoyance 2026 Fall Intensives 5 day course - 6 hours per day with additional 2 hour workshops PART 1: The Annoyance Training Center teaches students how to develop and maintain a strong individual voice in their improvisation. These intensives will address the initiation of a scene; developing and maintaining strong characters; learning to hold on to your initial choices while at the same time, expanding on (heightening) the choices you have made; and learning to become deft at creating and weaving unexpected, character-based choices into your work. Annoyance classes challenge the conventional wisdom of the rules of improvisation, examining what’s behind them and how they came to be in the first place. Simply put, we make you the best improviser YOU can be. All of our intensive teachers are ensemble members who have learned and performed with The Annoyance, and thus have first hand experience with the way we approach improvisation. PART 2: Took the Intensive Part 1 and want more? Want to go further with your exploration of our philosophy? This is for you! With this intensive sequel, we go deeper into the teachings of The Annoyance, tackling things like context, group scene work, ensemble and much more. If you can only take one week, take part 1 . In The Annoyance Intensives, we try to cover all of the information in The Annoyance's AP1-5 program each day - AP1 on Monday, AP2 on Tuesday, and so on. These classes meet for 20 hours over 8 weeks. In these intensives, we're trying to cram that info into 6 hours over one day. A truly impossible task. So, part 2 was created as a way to convey more of the information we couldn't get to in part 1, while also allowing instructors to get deeper into concepts they're passionate about, knowing the students all have at least part 1 under their belt. classes.title: Writing 1
classes.description: Writing 1 is for any student who is looking to start the journey of writing to and for a Second City-style revue. This class is the foundation for scenic comedy writing, a guide for utilizing an improviser’s skill set for rapid creation, and learning writing refinement. The concepts and tools provided in Writing 1 are the building blocks for being able to quickly and creatively write to and for a Second City-style revue. Every comedic writer must have the knowledge and skills that are offered in this course. Writing 1 teaches students how to write comedic scenes that could be found in a Second City-style revue by exploring different scenic set-ups, character dialogue, genres and point-of-view. Students use improv as a tool for character and dialogue generation. Writing 1 class time includes a combination of solo exercises and collaborative exercises to build skills in brainstorming, comedic structure and character development. Students also gain a Second City perspective on theatrical writing – including observation, idea generation, dialogue, and character creation. Feedback focuses on ‘what works’ in terms of the structural building blocks. Class time is active, positive and encouraging. Students practice concepts through a series of weekly homework assignments. Any student interested in creating Second City-style comedic writing or interested in extending their improvisation abilities and experiences into writing should take Writing 1.

classes.title: Improv for Anxiety - Beginner
classes.description: Improv for Anxiety (IFAX) – Beginner A 7-week introduction to the art of improvisation and the tools it offers to help us deal with social anxiety. This innovative class uses ensemble-based improv to help people manage feelings of stress and anxiety. A Second City instructor will lead the class through a series of improv exercises that are specifically chosen to help students focus on the present, step outside of their comfort zones, and embrace the unknown in a welcoming and low-pressure environment. About The Second City’s Improv for Anxiety Program The Improv for Anxiety Program aims to build a nurturing space and student community where students can slowly explore the tenets of improvisation while receiving accommodations due to feelings of fear or anxiety. The Improv for Anxiety Program is not a substitute for therapy. Instructors focus on teaching improvisational skills such as listening, being others focused, taking risks, and working from a “Yes, And…” mindset.

classes.title: Improv for Actors 1
classes.description: In today’s entertainment industry, improv and the ability to work without a script has become an essential tool not only for rehearsal, but for performance on screen and stage, as well as auditioning. Improv for Actors 1 is for people interested in how to be a better actor. IFA-1 is geared toward both working and post-grad actors who are looking to expand their craft with skills of improvisation. It’s also a great option for people with a theater degree or background in the dramatic arts to have fun with new people in a new environment. IFA-1 covers the fundamental elements of scene work, creating environment, and scenic transformation. Students acquire skills that can be applied to scripted and non-scripted work. Students become more improvisational in written moments and more confident when they improvise. Students work on building ensemble, object work, and the essence of a good improvisational scene. Using games, exercises, and other play, students explore relationships, character motivation, and heightening techniques to create compelling scenes and characters purely out of improv. Improv for Actors 1 is for students with a theater degree, currently enrolled in a full-length collegiate theater program, or have the equivalent amount of professional training and/or experience. Students must submit an acting résumé for consideration to be placed in IFA-1. Please submit a résumé through our online application.

classes.title: Slapstick: Physical Comedy in Clowning & Improv w Anya Opshinsky (Sunday, July 26th, 2026)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 14 students. Slapstick: Physical Comedy in Clowning We’ll explore the craft of slapstick comedy with a focus on precision, timing, and physical clarity. We’ll work on how character fuels comedy, character matching, character dissonance - The “Straight Man” and The “Fool”. We’ll also cover tried-and-true slapstick classics like the double take, convincing trips, the invisible chair fall, chases, and well-timed collisions. All experience levels welcome. Drop-in Class: Sunday, July 26th, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM. Where: BCC Pig Pen (144 Boerum Street, Brooklyn) Teacher: Anya Opshinsky Bio: Anya Opshinsky is a German American mime, actor, director, improvisor, physical theater creator and teacher. Originally from NYC Anya moved to Paris to study physical theater at École Jacques Lecoq 8 years ago and has been creating, teaching and performing there ever since. Anya is the co-founder of Shift Shape Theater through which she creates and tours original shows the most recent of which is entitled "ROVERS: Two Mimes on the Run" which is currently touring around France. Anya has taught mime, mask-making, clown, physical theater and improv with HUGE Improv, MN, Vis à Vis Théâtre, Rise Comedy, Denver and Théâtre de Verre, Paris. She is the creator and producer of The Ultimate Set-Up that performs in Denver, Paris and NYC and works as a solo artist performing original drag, mask, burlesque and clown pieces in the Parisian cabaret circuit If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: Workshop: Don’t Call It Clown (Call It Character) w/ Deanna Fleysher aka Big D. (July 29th & 30th)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 20 students. Workshop: Don’t Call It Clown (Call It Character) (July 29th & 30th) an intensive workshop in character clown with Deanna Fleysher aka Big D from TV's "King Of Drag" Wanna be unforgettable? Connect with your audiences like never before! Make them love you forever! in this intensive workshop with a master of interactive/clown character comedy, you will embark on a journey based in clown and physical theater to birth and hone electrifying clown characters that audiences will love, rooted deeply in who you are. We will learn about your character clown's unique connection to the audience, their mission, and their special brand of vulnerable ferocity. In this intensive, expect the following! The beginning, or the deepening, of a clown character that is uniquely yours and unstoppably lovable, and actual material you can take out of this intensive and onto a stage Personalized feedback (and plenty of it) Discovery and exploration of your character's favorite games and physical/emotional "sweet spots" More understanding of what “stage magnetism” means for you, and how to really harness your performative power. A deeper connection to and understanding of your audience More wildness, more bravery, more vulnerability. Interpretive Dance. Laughing a lot. This workshop is ideal for performers of any kind, improvisers, clowns, stand-up comedians, actors, movers, and anyone who wants to find/hone their own magnetic, funny and brave persona to play through on stage. Two-Day Workshop: Wednesday, July 29th & Thursday, July 30th. 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM. Where: BCC Pig Pen Theater (144 Boerum Street, Brooklyn) Teacher: Deanna Fleysher Bio: ABOUT THE TEACHER: Deanna Fleysher aka Big D is a character comedian and clown with 20+ years experience in masculine impersonation and general idiocy. She has toured the world with her solo character clown show BUTT KAPINSKI , her new duo show CINEPRIXX , and her workshops in character, clown, and drag. She has directed/co-created over 30+ solo character clown shows, supporting artists from all over the world in finding and honing their own unstoppable characters. Most recently, she appeared on season 1 of KING OF DRAG , on Revry. She will eat your talent for breakfast. More at www.dontcallitclown.com If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: [Aug-Oct] Clown Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town w/ Hannah Mitchell (Aug-Oct '26) (Thursday)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town Clown. The Great Equalizer. "It's you, of course, the clown. And that's important." -Nader Farman Clown trains us to be fully present. Class curriculum includes exercises from Jacques Lecoq, Jean Taylor, Merry Conway, Nader Farman, Keith Johnstone and more, aiming to move us past ego and toward our shared humanity. Winter is coming. But class is joyful and warm! Come on out, try on the red nose. Recognize the universal shared experience that is clowning. This class culminates in a class show at the BCC Pig Pen. 7-Week Class: Thursday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, September 3rd - October 15th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: 8:30pm. Saturday, October 17th. BCC Pig Pen (144 Boerum St). Teacher: Hannah Mitchell Bio : Hannah Mitchell is a performer, theater-maker, and educator whose process is guided by the improvisational arts, especially clown, bouffon & being a human with a body. She regularly collaborates with various ensembles to make new works, including with her clown company, WüfPak , and the experimental theater collective, CHILD . She’s performed at venues around NYC including The Public Theater, The Brick, The Collapsable Hole, Lincoln Center, LifeWorld, The New Ohio, Theater Mitu, HERE Arts, Target Margin, and The Brooklyn Comedy Collective, among others. And regionally/internationally she’s perfromed at The Philadelphia Fringe Festival, Bard SummerScape, Princeton Seuls en Scène, & The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. In this class, you will learn that anything can be the clown's script. classes.title: [Aug-Oct] Clown Level 3: Ensemble w/ Jeff Seal (Aug-Oct '26) (Thursdays)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. You’ve started getting laughs as a clown but now how do you do it 6 days a week and twice on Saturdays for an audience that’s never even heard of clowning? In this class we’ll take the skills you learned in Clown 1 and 2 honing your clown persona and learn to create working acts as part of a fully realized show, collaboratively created by the ensemble. How does your clown solve a problem? How do you work as a company? What's the world you're going to set up with lots of clowns? You will further build your clown character by finding a costume that makes you look and feel stupid and start creating material by finding your own stupid clown logic. We’ll also explore “le jeu” and status in clown duos and trios, as well as building a thematic world that ties the whole show together. Come to class with ideas, costumes, props or anything that ignites your imagination. Students should be prepared to rehearse as they would in a professional ensemble process - with homework prepped outside of class and attending classes and the tech rehearsal on-time and present. This class culminates in two class shows on the Eris MainStage Prerequisite: Clown Level 2 at BCC or similar experience. 7-Week Class: Wednesday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, September 3rd - October 15th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. Eris MainStage (167 Graham Ave). Class Show: 7:00pm. Thursday, October 22nd. Eris MainStage (167 Graham Ave). Class Show: 7:00pm. Thursday, October 29th. Eris MainStage (167 Graham Ave). Teacher: Jeff Seal Bio : Jeff Seal is a graduate of the Clown Conservatory at the San Francisco Circus Center and studied Decroux-based corporeal mime at the Hippocampe Project in Paris. He performs regularly with his two-man clown act, Jeff and Buttons, makes comedy videos with Bankrukt Productions and is one of the founding artistic directors of Cloud City Theater and Art space. His films and documentaries have been published by The New York Times, The Discovery Channel, AJ+, gothamist.com and screened at The Tribeca Film Festival. Testimonials : Jeff is an incredible coach and director. I loved Jeff's pragmatic teaching style, which really lends itself to the Level 3 Clown course. I think his best strength as a teacher was meeting us where we were with whatever ideas/bits we brought in and helping us figure out what was working or not (and how it would be different performing in front of the audience than in front of the rest of the class). Jeff put us in position to learn how the skills we had picked up in the earlier levels could be applied to an act that would work in front of an audience. Jeff is really good at helping people find the game in their bits - this class is a great next step after Chris’ level 2. Jeff's an inspiring director with tons of funny and creative ideas. He helped us develop our ideas into much stronger and tighter bits, with many more games than we would have thought to include, while helping us think about structure so that all those games came together in a cohesive way without getting confusing or going all over the place. He also has a great mind for comedic possibility and guided us to discover entire bits from even the smallest seeds of ideas. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.description: Free Your Mind This class is limited to 16 students. Love the hell out of what’s happening onstage and you’re bound to have fun and fearless improvisation. This class will help remind you what you love about improv, slap you out of whatever may be holding you back, and cut out the naysaying voices in your head. We will look at how we sometimes bail on our choices out of a fear of making the “right or wrong choice” and to instead approach improv with a “fuck it/love it” mentality (because improv is one of the most wonderful things in the world if you don’t treat it too seriously). This class encourages your artistic freedom with methods to initiate scenes with an emotional point of view, to be specific and surprising in your choices, to support your scene partner, and to confidently navigate the vocabulary and concepts of long-form improv. This class will move quickly, keep you on your feet, and challenge you with personal feedback. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Improv OR students who have taken at least one full-length course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training can begin at this level. classes.title: [Aug-Oct] Improv Level 3: Advanced Scene Dynamics w/ John Randall (Aug-Oct '26) (Monday)
classes.description: Advanced Scenes This class is limited to 16 students. This class builds on the skills in Level 2, teaching students to be versatile ensemble players who can build long-form pieces organically while keeping the show and audience's experience in mind. By layering more complicated improv techniques and awareness onto already fearless play, students will learn how to authentically ground characters so they feel believable and use curve balls to keep scenes surprising. classes.description: This class is limited to 16 students. Structure (Gather Your Ingredients) Musical improv classes at BCC are taught at an accelerated pace compared to other schools. classes.description: The Building Blocks of Sketch This class is limited to 12 students. This class is for anyone interested in learning the process of writing sketch comedy. classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. You’ve started getting laughs as a clown but now how do you do it 6 days a week and twice on Saturdays for an audience that’s never even heard of clowning? In this class we’ll take the skills you learned in Clown 1 and 2 honing your clown persona and learn to create working acts as part of a fully realized show, collaboratively created by the ensemble. How does your clown solve a problem? How do you work as a company? What's the world you're going to set up with lots of clowns? You will further build your clown character by finding a costume that makes you look and feel stupid and start creating material by finding your own stupid clown logic. We’ll also explore “le jeu” and status in clown duos and trios, as well as building a thematic world that ties the whole show together. Come to class with ideas, costumes, props or anything that ignites your imagination. Students should be prepared to rehearse as they would in a professional ensemble process - with homework prepped outside of class and attending classes and the tech rehearsal on-time and present. Eris MainStage Teacher: Jeff Seal Bio : Jeff Seal is a graduate of the Clown Conservatory at the San Francisco Circus Center and studied Decroux-based corporeal mime at the Hippocampe Project in Paris. He performs regularly with his two-man clown act, Jeff and Buttons, makes comedy videos with Bankrukt Productions and is one of the founding artistic directors of Cloud City Theater and Art space. His films and documentaries have been published by The New York Times, The Discovery Channel, AJ+, gothamist.com and screened at The Tribeca Film Festival. Testimonials : Jeff is an incredible coach and director. I loved Jeff's pragmatic teaching style, which really lends itself to the Level 3 Clown course. I think his best strength as a teacher was meeting us where we were with whatever ideas/bits we brought in and helping us figure out what was working or not (and how it would be different performing in front of the audience than in front of the rest of the class). Jeff put us in position to learn how the skills we had picked up in the earlier levels could be applied to an act that would work in front of an audience. Jeff is really good at helping people find the game in their bits - this class is a great next step after Chris’ level 2. Jeff's an inspiring director with tons of funny and creative ideas. He helped us develop our ideas into much stronger and tighter bits, with many more games than we would have thought to include, while helping us think about structure so that all those games came together in a cohesive way without getting confusing or going all over the place. He also has a great mind for comedic possibility and guided us to discover entire bits from even the smallest seeds of ideas. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: [July-Aug] Improv Level 3: Advanced Scene Dynamics w/ Tej Khanna (July-August '26) (Thursday)
classes.description: Advanced Scenes This class is limited to 16 students. This class builds on the skills in Level 2, teaching students to be versatile ensemble players who can build long-form pieces organically while keeping the show and audience's experience in mind. By layering more complicated improv techniques and awareness onto already fearless play, students will learn how to authentically ground characters so they feel believable and use curve balls to keep scenes surprising. We’ll explore the dynamics that make up three-person and group scenes and how to augment and balance multiple points of focus onstage. And we’ll learn to stay out of our heads while balancing it all. In addition to learning advanced techniques, this class will encourage you to lean into the areas you shy away from and own what makes you unique as a performer through personal notes and individual challenges. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite: BCC Level 2 “Free Your Mind.” 7-Week Class: Thursday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 16th - August 27th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Friday, August 28th. 8:30pm. BCC Pig Pen. Teacher: Tej Khanna Bio: Tej Khanna is a New York based comedian. He has been performing improv for over ten years. His trio Chicken Big has been featured on America's Got Talent, SF Sketchfest, headlined Fun And Dumb Improv Festival and Oberlin College Improv Conference. He has taught improv at Oberlin College, NYU and The New School. Testimonials: Tej had such a unique point of view. He’s super talented and funny and talks about improv in a way I haven't heard before but that was super useful Tej made all the exercises super approachable! I felt safe and supported in trying new things which made taking comedic risks all the more valuable:) please have more tej workshops/classes! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: [July-Aug] Musical Improv Level 1: Structure w/ Daniel Tepper (July-August '26) (Tuesdays)
classes.description: This class is limited to 16 students. Structure (Gather Your Ingredients) Musical improv classes at BCC are taught at an accelerated pace compared to other schools. You will be performing a fully fledged musical by the end of level 1. You already know how to improvise, but to music? YES YOU CAN! This class will introduce improvisers with at least 1 level of experience how to create fully formed narrative musicals on the spot. We will begin each class with various rhythmic, rhyming, and listening, exercises, then delve deep into each element of narrative structure. Students will learn how to create strong protagonists, antagonists, charm scenes, and finales, while building a toolbox of musical techniques including fugues, and of course classic chorus-verse structures. Strong singing voices are not required! Just a willingness to unleash your inner diva in a joyful and supportive atmosphere. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Improv OR students who have taken at least one full-length course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training can begin at this level. Please write your prior experience in the form when registering. Note: BCC’s Musical Improv 1 culminates in a full-length Improvised Musical so we require a prerequisite of a full-length Improv 1 class. Our musical improv classes move at a faster pace compared to most other theaters' programs. This is an advanced class, so we do not allow skipping to Musical Improv 2. Trust us, you will be challenged :) 7-Week Class: Tuesdays, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 14th - August 25th, 2026 Please note: This class may end between 9:30 - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Thursday, August 27th. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: Daniel Tepper Accompanist: TBD Bio: Daniel Tepper is an actor and musical comedian based in New York City. As a musical improviser, he has performed all over New York, toured the country, and performed internationally with shows including Baby Wants Candy ; Your Love, Our Musical ; Shitzprobe; Shamilton ; Broadway’s Next Hit Musical ; What Town? The Musical ; the Off-Broadway workshop of The Greatest Musical Never Written; and his long running group Vern (featured on multiple Audible productions). Favorite theatre credits include shows at the Atlantic Theater Company, NYC Fringe Festival Encores Series, Williamstown Theater Festival, Edinburgh Festival Fringe, TheatreSquared, and The 92nd Street Y. Daniel’s strangest credits include acting in a commercial for a straw that sucks snot out of a baby’s nose, and playing a freestyling hot dog named “Lil Doggie” for Sonic Drive Thru’s snap chat account. He is very excited to be teaching at BCC! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: [July-Aug] Stand-Up Level 2: Solidifying Your Set w/ JoAnn Schinderle (July-August '26) (Mondays)
classes.description: Solidifying Your Set This class is limited to 12 students. Building on Level 1 "Finding Your Voice", Stand-up: Level 2 is all about solidifying your choices to develop your voice and find your niche. By focusing on each individual’s strengths, this course will focus on how to craft a clean set, host and put together a show, and develop tricks to solidify your own voice. We will workshop material as a group and I’ll give personalized feedback every week. If you want to take your sets to a deeper level and have a better understanding of what makes you funny, take this class. Class will culminate in a performance of each student’s 10-minute sets, leading you to the beginnings of your very own stand alone show. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Stand-Up OR students who have taken at least one full-length stand-up course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training or performance experience can begin at this level. Class Show: Saturday, August 29th. 8:30pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: JoAnn Schinderle Bio: JoAnn Schinderle is a Brooklyn-based standup, actress, and writer. Getting her start in Portland, OR she was voted PDX's Funniest Person by peers and industry, as well as an ‘Undisputable Genius of Comedy’ by local papers. A seasoned opener for touring acts including Fortune Feimster, Tom Papa, and Lena Dunham (‘14 book tour), JoAnn has also been featured nationally in festivals such as; SF Sketchfest, Bridgetown, Bumbershoot, B4UDIE, & 10,000 Laughs. In 2019, JoAnn wrote and produced an album of period jokes titled ‘Dark Jeans', as a middle finger to the notion that 'period jokes aren’t funny'. Turns out, they are? Dark Jeans sat #1 on iTunes during the week of its release with Comedy Dynamics. From there, she's a proud featured talking head on the 2024 MSNBC documentary 'Periodical' featuring Gloria Steinem. Schinderle serves her day job duties as a live audience host for network TV, Venue Manager for UCB LA (‘22-’23) & UCB NYC (‘23-24’), and her most favorite job of writing commissioned erotica under her not-yet-famous company, ‘JoJosNosNos’. Testimonials: Jo created a supportive and fun environment in which to explore ideas that feel too intimidating to try out in other stand up contexts! JoAnn is awesome! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.description: This class is limited to 10 students. In this 7 session class, students will learn comedy pilot basics like pilot structure, character and story development, writers room skills like pitching, punch - ups, screenplay software and formatting. Students are not allowed to bring in old projects, but come to class “blank slate,” where e ach writer will create new characters and worlds culminating in a final read through of samples from e ach writers' pilots created during the class. Seven class sessions over 7 weeks. The writers room vibes will be elevated by participation from James Livingston, Hollywood script coo r dinator who will act as writers assistant to the class. Class will be led by Peabody Award winning and 4x Emmy nominated writer and producer, Rich Talarico (Key & Peele, SNL, Tonight S h ow, MADtv and Review). 7-Week Class: Thursdays, 7:00PM - 10:00 PM EST, September 10th - October 22nd , 2026 Class may end between 9:30 - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. This class does not end in a class show. Teacher: Rich Talarico Bio: Rich is an actor, comedian, improviser and established writer/producer best known for his work on Comedy Central’s Key & Peele. Rich wrote K&P favorites Substitute Teacher, Racist Zombies, Karim and Jahar, Hatz, Teaching Center, Obama Greets, Black Ice and many more. Rich wrote and produced on MADtv, The Tonight Show w. Jay Leno, Saturday Night Live, and the critically acclaimed, Review. Rich took his first improv class from Stephen Colbert at Chicago’s Second City. Rich went on to tour with Second City's National Touring Company and later co-created five original sketch comedy revues with Second City's Etc., and Main Stage Companies. While in Chicago, Rich was a performer and teacher at Improv Olympic (Now iO) under the direction of Charna Halpern and the late, Del Close. As an actor, Rich has appeared in featured roles on Late Night with Conan O’Brien, MADtv, the feature film High Fidelity, and A&W Root Beer’s classic TV ad, “Mr. Dumass.” Talarico’s extensive experience in the comedy industry has made him a sought-after teacher, writer and consultant. — More at richtalarico.com Testimonials: “Rich's work on "Key & Peele" is nothing short of legendary.” — Jordan Peele “As a teacher, Rich Talarico brings Lakers legendary coach Phil Jackson to mind. A cerebral educator who brings out the best in people.” — Keith “Lucky” Lehrer original drummer of “Circle Jerks” If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: [Sept-Oct] Marrying Game & Deal w/ Philip Markle and Akmal Tajihan (Sept-Oct '26) (Sunday)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. Combine multiple philosophies to become a better improviser! Giving yourself a well-rounded improv education by taking classes at different institutions is critical in growing your talent, education, and joy. But sometimes it is difficult to figure out how to combine these philosophies together into a successful scene. That is what this class is all about! Learn to bring the superpowers of different schools into one powerful set. We will focus on pairing the BCC’s f*ck it / love it philosophy and playing from a deal with a game-based approach to finding and playing with the unusual thing. We will cover core BCC concepts like managing surprise + believability & byways and highways with game-based methodology of “the voice of reason” and mapping beats out over a long-form show. The class will culminate in a simplified longform Harold. Learn to play fearlessly from a deal while honing the game of the scene - and keeping it all loose and fun. Prerequisite: Completion of both UCB 201 and BCC Level 2 improv (no exceptions) 5-Week Class: Sunday, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, September 20th - October 18th, 2026 This class may end between 5:30 PM and 6:00 PM depending on enrollment. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: 8:30pm. Sunday, October 18th. BCC Pig Pen (144 Boerum St). Teachers: Philip Markle and Akmal Tajihan Philip’s Bio: Philip Markle is the Founder and Artistic Director of The Brooklyn Comedy Collective. Philip trained in improv in Chicago at The Annoyance, iO, and Second City before moving to NYC in 2013 to launch The Annoyance Theater NY, where he acted as its Director for four years before founding the BCC. He was named “Top 5 Queer Business Leaders” by Time Out NY in 2023 and a “Power Player in Arts in Culture” by AMNY in 2024. The New York Times wrote, “Philip Markle, who founded [the BCC] in 2018, has done creative work building an audience around a dizzying array of shows.” He has taught thousands of students of all ages internationally, from Bali to Berlin, for over 15 years. Performance highlights include musically improvising with Baby Wants Candy in Chicago and BLANK: The Musical Off-Broadway. As a storyteller, he has performed at The Moth and The Paper Machete - and is a writer on Medium.com with 400k+ reads. He executive produced, wrote, and acted in the digital shorts series "The Annoyance Presents" on IFC's Comedy Crib. Philip first performed his show SPARKLE HOUR to rave reviews in the 2013 NY International Fringe Festival and has regularly produced it since, including multiple runs at Joe's Pub and as part of the 2021-2023 NY Comedy Festivals. You can listen to his queer comedy songs on your favorite streaming music platform under the name “Philip Sparkle.” Akmal’s Bio: Akmal Tajihan is an NYC Comedian and Improv teacher. He has served as a faculty member at Upright Citizens Brigade Theater teaching for the past 2 years and has been doing improv/ comedy for over 10. You can see him perform weekly with his group Duchovny on TGIF 7pm at UCB! He also co-hosts the show WHAT THE HELL monthly at UCB. He’s probably the most midsized guy you’ll ever see. classes.title: [Sept-Oct] Physical Storytelling 101 w/ Broken Box Mime Theater (Sept-Oct '26) (Saturday)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 14 students. Broken Box Mime Theater (BKBX) invites artists of all backgrounds to join in exploring the audience's imagination as medium. From the perspective of BKBX's unique brand of mime based in American narrative style, participants will look at body language, basic mime technique, staging metaphor, efficiency in storytelling, and devised process. How can precision, efficiency, and specificity in physical storytelling help to craft our comedy? Over the course of the series, students will craft mime pieces that will be shared in a final showing in December. 5-Week Class: Saturday, 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, September 12th - October 17th, 2026 This class skips Saturday, September 26th. This class may end between 2:30 PM and 3 PM depending on enrollment. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: 7:00pm. Sunday, October 18th. Eris Deep Space (167 Graham Ave). Teacher: Broken Box Mime Theater Bio: This course will be taught by a member of BKBX. Broken Box Mime Theater (BKBX) is a collaborative theater company that performs original work entirely through movement. Our mission is to activate the imaginations of our audiences, contemporize the art of mime, and remind us all of the power of simple storytelling. Based in Brooklyn and founded in 2011, our version of “mime, redefined” blends techniques of French pantomime, cinema, performance art, and contemporary theater. The artists who call BKBX their artistic home believe in making innovative, provocative theater that erases the spoken language barrier and champions the power of simplicity in performance. Photo by Bjorn Bolinder Student Testimonials: Becky made everyone feel welcome and empowered to improve. Becky was absolutely fantastic in every way! Becky taught us so many tools and tricks to story telling through physical movement, while also being so fun and supportive! Becky is such an incredible teacher! Too many strengths to pick one, but a couple that stand out are her unbridled enthusiasm for mime and her capacity to share that with others. She taught us that mime is grounded in trust between the audience and performer, and she brought that same ethos to class. She's the rare teacher who realizes that how you teach has a bigger impact than what you teach, and it's refreshing to be in an education space that starts from a foundation of embracing that we all learn in different ways, and that's great! There's a curiosity to Becky's teaching that's really encouraging - asking students what we want from the class, and adapting her style and lessons to suit us. Class felt like a team effort to learn and discover together. Despite her obvious deep knowledge of the subject, Becky's was still very clearly also interested in our experience and ideas - it made for a fantastic environment for creating and sharing work. Her obvious joy when something clicked for us was infectious. It's a welcome change from traditional education models where an 'expert' relays knowledge to students. Everything about her teaching style makes it clear she's as eager to keep learning and exploring as we are - no gatekeeping in site. Honestly, I think more teachers should take Becky's class to inform their own pedagogy! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: [Sept-Oct] Physical Storytelling 201: Creation Workshop w/ Broken Box Mime Theater (Sept-Oct '26) (Tuesdays)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 14 students. Craft a mime piece from scratch! Designed as a Level 2 to the intro class "Physical Storytelling" with Broken Box Mime, this next-level class will continue the exploration of contemporary mime as a storytelling form. What stories can the body tell, when words, props, costume pieces, and sets are stripped away? What can the limitlessness of the audience's imagination teach us about communication? Students will develop a brief comedic mime piece over the course of the class, integrating multiple rounds of brainstorming, writing, and feedback, all in embodied practice. classes.title: [Sept-Oct] Scenes & Notes w/ James Dwyer (Sept-Oct '26) (Monday)

Over these four sessions everyone will receive personal feedback on their improv to help sharpen their skills and level up their play. We will focus purely on scenes, notes, and side-coaching to figure out what's working, what isn't, and what you can do to make sure you're having more fun onstage. Eris Deep Space (167 Graham Ave). Teacher: James Dwyer Bio: James is an improviser, actor, and comedian who has been improvising in NYC since 2008.
